era: San Francisco/Seattle. World-music-meets-electronic. Global instruments with electronic processing. City of Tribes Records.

location.origin: San Francisco, California. Connected to Seattle/PNW through performances and scene overlap.
location.formative: Bay Area world music and electronic scenes. Global instrument traditions meeting electronic processing technology.
location.career: Trance Mission -- world/electronic group blending oud, tabla, didgeridoo, and other global instruments with electronic processing. City of Tribes Records. World/Electronic at Genre Stage 74. E=3. Second degree of Quadrant II.

associated_musicians.name: Sky Cries Mary
associated_musicians.note: Both blend world elements with electronics. Sky Cries Mary uses world influences within an ethereal framework; Trance Mission makes world instruments primary.

associated_musicians.name: The Postal Service
associated_musicians.note: Both electronic-centered. Postal Service = electronic pop. Trance Mission = world/electronic. Different traditions.

associated_musicians.name: Philip Aaberg
associated_musicians.note: Both incorporate world music elements. Aaberg through Western piano; Trance Mission uses global instruments directly.

associated_musicians.name: Chong the Nomad
associated_musicians.note: Both electronic. Chong is beat-driven experimental; Trance Mission is world-instrument-driven.

associated_musicians.name: Hovercraft
associated_musicians.note: Consecutive degrees. Hovercraft uses electronics for obliteration; Trance Mission for cultural bridge-building.

theory_nodes.concept: The global weave (ninety-third acoustic element -- Trance Mission, multiple world instruments from different traditions woven through electronic processing, polyphonic, no single tradition dominates, oud + tabla + didgeridoo + electronics)
examples: Trance Mission (degree 92) -- oud, tabla, didgeridoo woven through electronic processing. The global weave.
Contrast with the sustained crush (degree 91, Hovercraft) -- monolithic wall. The global weave is polyphonic tapestry.
The engine moves from monolith (91) to tapestry (92). From crushing uniformity to woven diversity.
Multiple traditions interlocking. No single tradition dominates. Electronic processing as connective tissue.
theory_nodes.description: The global weave is the ninety-third acoustic element. Multiple world instruments woven through electronics. Polyphonic. Tapestry not monolith.

ancestors: The sustained crush (degree 91, Hovercraft) -- monolithic. The global weave is polyphonic.
The ambient drift (degree 90, Sky Cries Mary) -- world influences. The global weave makes them primary.
genealogy.relationship: Ninety-three acoustic elements across ninety-two degrees. Track element 94 at degree 93.

theory_nodes.concept: The cross-pollinator (ninety-third artist-city pattern -- operating across cities and traditions, carrying elements between scenes, resident of the space between traditions, dissolving borders)
examples: Trance Mission (degree 92) -- San Francisco/Seattle, world/electronic. The cross-pollinator.
The cross-pollinator belongs to no single scene because they belong to all of them.
Carrying the oud into the electronic club and the sampler into the world music festival.
Creating new music by dissolving the borders that separate old music.
theory_nodes.description: The cross-pollinator is the ninety-third artist-city pattern. Operating across cities and traditions. Dissolving borders. Carrying elements between scenes.

ancestors: The spousal satellite (degree 91, Hovercraft) -- biographical orbit. The cross-pollinator is geographical and cultural orbit.
The parallel scene (degree 90, Sky Cries Mary) -- operating alongside. The cross-pollinator operates between.
genealogy.relationship: Ninety-three artist-city patterns across ninety-two degrees. Track pattern 94 at degree 93.

theory_nodes.concept: World/Electronic at Genre Stage 74 -- global instruments + electronic processing; neither traditional world nor conventional electronic; hybrid that dissolves the boundary
examples: Trance Mission (degree 92) -- World/Electronic at Genre Stage 74.
Global instruments (oud, tabla, didgeridoo) + electronic processing.
Distinguished from ethereal/electronic (90) and drone/noise (91).
The first entry to explicitly fuse world music traditions with electronic technology as core aesthetic.
theory_nodes.description: World/Electronic at Genre Stage 74. Global instruments meet electronics. Hybrid. Dissolves boundaries between traditions.

genealogy.relationship: Genre Stage 74 at degree 92. Three consecutive electronic-inflected genres (72-73-74). Track at 93.

theory_nodes.concept: E=3 at degree 92 -- immersive but accessible; world/electronic hybrid invites the listener into unfamiliar traditions through familiar electronic textures
examples: Trance Mission (degree 92) -- E=3. Immersive accessibility.
Electronics make unfamiliar instruments accessible.
E=3 rebounds from E=2 at degree 91.
theory_nodes.description: E=3 at degree 92. Immersive accessibility. Electronics as gateway to unfamiliar traditions.

ancestors: E=2 at degree 91 (Hovercraft). Rebounds to E=3 at 92.

theory_nodes.concept: The electronic continuum (degrees 90-92) -- three consecutive electronic-inflected degrees with different relationships: atmosphere (90), obliteration (91), bridge-building (92)
examples: Sky Cries Mary (90) -- electronics for atmosphere.
Hovercraft (91) -- electronics for obliteration.
Trance Mission (92) -- electronics for cultural bridge-building.
Three degrees, three uses. The electronic spectrum in three entries.
theory_nodes.description: The electronic continuum: atmosphere (90), obliteration (91), bridge-building (92). Three uses of electronics in three degrees.

ancestors: Degrees 90-92 form a micro-arc of electronic usage.
genealogy.relationship: Track whether degree 93 continues or breaks the electronic pattern.

theory_nodes.concept: City of Tribes Records -- San Francisco label; world/electronic focus; part of the Bay Area's world music infrastructure
examples: Trance Mission (degree 92) -- City of Tribes Records.
San Francisco label. World/electronic focus.
The label map extends beyond Seattle: first SF-based label.
theory_nodes.description: City of Tribes Records. San Francisco label. World/electronic focus. The label map extends beyond Seattle.

ancestors: Seattle labels: Sub Pop, Barsuk, K Records, Rockville, Tag, DGC. Now City of Tribes (SF).
genealogy.relationship: Track label geography. Non-Seattle labels enter the map.

cross_references.relationship: World music/electronic. Both blend world elements with electronics. Sky Cries Mary uses world influences within ethereal framework; Trance Mission makes world instruments primary.
Electronic. Both electronic-centered. Postal Service = electronic pop. Trance Mission = world/electronic. Different traditions feeding into electronic transformation.
World influences. Both incorporate world music. Aaberg through Western piano; Trance Mission uses global instruments directly. Western mediation vs direct global voice.
Electronic. Both electronic. Chong is beat-driven experimental; Trance Mission is world-instrument-driven. Different sources into electronic transformation.
Electronic era. Consecutive degrees. Hovercraft uses electronics for obliteration (drone/noise); Trance Mission for bridge-building (world/electronic). Destruction vs connection.
